lesson.menu_desc: Check in at the front desk and make polite requests with I'd like... and Would you mind...? -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- Hotel Check-in -- at the front desk, stating what you need and asking favors politely
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rubens Tofolo | Aula 18 -- Hotel Check-in

listenings.text: Good evening, and welcome to the Garden Plaza Hotel. This is a short message about your stay. Breakfast is served in the lobby restaurant from six thirty to ten in the morning. The fitness room and the pool are on the second floor, and they are open until eleven at night. Our amenities also include free wifi in every room and in the lobby. If you would like a wake-up call, please dial zero for the front desk, and we will be happy to arrange it. Checkout is at noon. If you need a later checkout, just let us know, and we will do our best. We hope you enjoy your stay with us. If there is anything you need, our team is here twenty-four hours a day.

When I travel abroad for a medical conference, I always book a quiet hotel near the venue. I usually arrive late and tired, so a smooth check-in really matters to me. At the front desk, I give my name and say I have a reservation. Then I ask a few simple things. I always say, I would like a room on a high floor, please, because it is quieter. And if I have an early meeting, I ask, would you mind giving me a wake-up call at six? I have learned that hotel staff are very helpful when you are polite and clear. A good night of sleep before a long conference day makes all the difference for me.

extra_audio.text: Here is what I do when I arrive at a hotel. First, I go to the front desk and say I have a reservation. Then I give my name and show my passport. After that, I leave a deposit and the staff give me my key card. Next, I ask for a wake-up call and a room on a high floor. Finally, I go up to my room and check that everything works.
